title: Moscow's Bauman University, the secretive school training Russian hackers
subhead: According to 2,000 documents obtained by a consortium of international media, including Le Monde, the prestigious Bauman Moscow State Technical University has been discreetly training Russian military intelligence officers, particularly hackers.

title: France deploys aircraft carrier to Gulf of Aden, ready to act in Strait of Hormuz
Rubio steps up as defender of Trump's erratic Iran policy
Hantavirus: Many unknowns surround an 'unprecedented and worrying' outbreak
subhead: The WHO has sought to reassure the public about the risk of a pandemic. A strain which is transmissible between humans via saliva droplets was identified in three patients on Wednesday, on a cruise ship bound for the Canary Islands.

title: Gaza police become targets for Israeli army
subhead: Police forces under Hamas's authority are regularly targeted by the Israeli army. Israel describes these as preventive strikes, which target officers who also serve in the Islamist movement's armed wing. Civil society actors, on the other hand, accuse Israel of seeking to plunge the territory into chaos.

title: Unstoppable PSG withstands Bayern to reach second consecutive Champions League final
subhead: Eight days after a spectacular victory at the Parc des Princes, Paris Saint-Germain secured qualification in a return leg that felt like a battle on German soil.

title: Welsh nationalists poised for historic election breakthrough
subhead: For the first time in its history, the pro-independence Welsh party Plaid Cymru could top the Welsh Parliament in Thursday's election. The surge reflects not just disillusionment with rivals but a deeper frustration at years of being sidelined by London.

Scottish elections: Reform UK capitalizes on anti-migrant protests in Falkirk
subhead: As regional elections take place on Thursday, May 7, in Scotland and Wales, far-right activists have managed to rally broad support around slogans hostile to asylum seekers in a region previously known for being welcoming.

title: Laure Ferrari, the Frenchwoman whispering in Nigel Farage's ear
subhead: Little-known in France, Ferrari works as an adviser to her partner Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, currently leading the polls in the UK.

title: French architects of Iran nuclear deal watch Trump unravel their work
subhead: French diplomats who helped negotiate the 2015 Vienna agreement, aimed to prevent Tehran from acquiring nuclear weapons, now watch bitterly as decisions by the Trump administration unravel their work.
